stimulus: The Tirumalai Nayak murals, from seventeenth-century South India, depict more than 60 court scenes across the long inner walls of a palace audience hall. They were likely produced by teams of painters working in vertical sections and then aligning the resulting panels along the wall. It’s plausible that the workshop had not previously executed murals on so long a wall, and some art historians claim that a close examination of the seams—the places where the panels meet—suggests that the painters refined their alignment process over the course of production. For instance, the earliest seam completed shows a clear mismatch of the painted border between the two panels, whereas the later seams are nearly imperceptible.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It identifies the figures and events depicted in the Tirumalai Nayak murals.
It compares the Tirumalai Nayak murals with other murals from seventeenth-century South India.
It supports an argument about the painters who produced the Tirumalai Nayak murals.
It describes how researchers determined where the Tirumalai Nayak murals were produced.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it most accurately describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole. The text discusses the Tirumalai Nayak murals, suggesting that the painters who produced them may not have worked on so long a wall before and refined their alignment process over time. The last sentence provides an example that supports this argument: the earliest seam shows a clear mismatch, whereas later seams are nearly imperceptible. The underlined sentence thus supports an argument about the painters.

Choice A is incorrect because the example in the last sentence concerns the seams between panels, not the people or events depicted. Choice B is incorrect because the last sentence compares early and late seams within the same set of murals, not different murals from the same period. Choice D is incorrect because the last sentence does not address how researchers determined the murals’ location.
